preamble: The deaffrication of the voiceless retroflex affricate [ʈʂ] into a voiceless retroflex fricative [ʂ]. This is a common lenition process in which the initial stop closure is lost.

examples.note: Synchronic variation in some dialects where affricates simplify to fricatives.
Final stage of retroflex stop lenition.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
